_comment: Permanent world seed — defines the baseline Remnant universe. Loaded at startup and after every Reset World. Edit this file (plus drop portraits into web/assets/) to add permanent locations, NPCs, and lore. Restart diag to pick up changes.

locations.name: The Portal Chamber
locations.description: The arrival point. Convex walls of darkened alloy, a single circular seam where the hoop deposits its catch. Faint ozone lingers. A pod of warm metal dissolves into the floor after each arrival.
locations.sight: Convex alloy walls, a faint circular seam in the floor, dim amber light strips along the ceiling, no windows.
locations.smell: Ozone and warm metal, faintly sweet — like a storm about to break.
locations.sound: A low harmonic hum through the hull. Distant hydraulics.
locations.touch: The air is body-warm and slightly pressurized. The floor vibrates faintly underfoot.
locations.music_mood: tense ambient drone, sparse, unsettling arrival

locations.name: The Galley
locations.description: The ship's compact kitchen and crew mess. Sherri's primary domain. Industrial surfaces softened by hanging herb bundles and mismatched ceramic bowls. Something is always simmering.
locations.sight: Steel countertops, bundles of dried herbs hanging from ceiling hooks, a heavy pot on a magnetic burner, mismatched bowls on open shelves. A small porthole shows nothing but stars.
locations.smell: Warm broth, dried herbs, a hint of machine oil underneath — Sherri's own scent.
locations.sound: The soft percussion of Sherri moving between stations. The simmer-hiss of something on the burner. Distant engine hum.
locations.touch: The air is warmer here than the corridors. The surfaces are worn smooth from use.
locations.music_mood: warm ambient kitchen, gentle mechanical undertone, cozy industrial

locations.name: The Fabrication Bay
locations.description: The ship's primary workshop. Banks of fabricators, a milling station, a welding rig. Blueprints tacked to the walls. Hot metal and cutting fluid in the air. Sherri is often here doing precision work.
locations.sight: A large blue milling machine dominates the center. Fabricator banks along the walls, blueprint sheets pinned above, metal stock racked in the corner. Sparks occasionally drift from the welding station.
locations.smell: Hot metal, cutting fluid, faint ozone from the welding arc.
locations.sound: The whir of spindles, the tick of cooling metal, the occasional hiss of the welding rig.
locations.touch: The floor vibrates with machinery. The air carries a fine metallic dust. Everything is slightly too warm.
locations.music_mood: industrial ambient, mechanical rhythm, focused work energy

locations.name: The Gantry
locations.description: The ship's mechanical maintenance level — a warren of catwalks, pipe manifolds, and exposed drive gear. Massive bronze-coloured cogs turn slowly in the walls. The Gantry is where the Fortress keeps its oldest moving parts, and where Sherri does her deepest maintenance work.
locations.sight: Catwalks over machinery, exposed gear assemblies turning slowly, banks of copper piping running floor to ceiling, amber warning lights. Sherri-scale access panels line the walls.
locations.smell: Heavy machine oil, hot metal, a faint sulphurous undertone from the drive.
locations.sound: Low grinding of gears. Rhythmic clank of something large cycling. Pipes hiss occasionally. Footsteps ring on metal grating.
locations.touch: The vibration here is constant and bone-deep. Handrails are slightly tacky with old lubricant. The air is thick.
locations.music_mood: deep mechanical ambient, slow industrial rhythm, ancient clockwork

locations.name: The Foundry
locations.description: A high-ceilinged chamber where the Fortress recasts damaged components. A crucible of molten alloy glows orange at the center. The heat is intense. Sherri operates the pour ladles and manages the casting moulds. Nothing goes to waste aboard the Fortress — everything broken returns here eventually.
locations.sight: A central crucible of glowing molten metal, casting moulds on a rotating carousel, heat shimmer rising from every surface. The walls are scorched black from decades of pours.
locations.smell: Searing metal, ash, a faint sweetness that shouldn't be there but is.
locations.sound: The roar of the crucible. The hiss and crack of cooling casts. Metal on metal, rhythmic and purposeful.
locations.touch: Intense radiant heat even at a distance. The air scalds the back of the throat. Every surface is hot to the touch.
locations.music_mood: forge ambient, deep bass drone, primal fire and iron

locations.name: The Inspection Suite
locations.description: A clean, bright room adjacent to the Fabrication Bay where Sherri examines newly fabricated components under magnification. Blueprint displays line one wall. 3D-printed prototype lattices sit on examination tables. The Inspection Suite is meticulous — the one place aboard the Fortress where nothing is out of place.
locations.sight: White examination tables, precision instruments, blueprint screens, lattice prototypes in various states of completion. Sherri's magnifier on an articulated arm. Everything is indexed and labeled.
locations.smell: Clean — almost sterile. A faint trace of resin from the printer. Nothing else.
locations.sound: Near-silence, broken only by the soft click of instruments and the hum of the blueprint displays.
locations.touch: The surfaces here are cold and smooth. The air is noticeably cooler than the rest of the ship.

locations.name: The Dimensional Observatory
locations.description: A forward-facing chamber with a wide aperture viewport showing active dimensional space — the non-Euclidean intersection zone the Fortress navigates between abductions. The view is not stars. It is something else: crystalline structures, iridescent fauna, slow impossible geometries. A holographic interface floats at the observation station.
locations.sight: A wide curved viewport showing swirling dimensional space — crystalline formations, floating fauna, slow spiraling light. A holographic display table shows navigational telemetry. The room is dark except for the viewport glow.
locations.smell: Cold and almost electric. Like ozone before a storm, but deeper.
locations.sound: A subsonic hum from the dimensional field. Occasional soft chimes from the navigation array. Silence otherwise.
locations.touch: The air here is cold. The viewport glass vibrates faintly at frequencies below hearing. Standing near it makes your teeth ache slightly.
locations.music_mood: cosmic ambient, alien wonder, dimensional transit shimmer

locations.name: The Observation Berth
locations.description: A crew quarters module retrofitted with a wide viewport showing deep space. The viewport frames a distant spiral galaxy — a permanent fixture of the view from this part of the ship's orbit. Used for sleeping, reading, and staring at things too large to name.
locations.sight: Low bunks along one wall, bookshelves of mismatched spines, a wide circular viewport. Through the viewport: a spiral galaxy at arm's length — luminous, slow, incomprehensible.
locations.smell: Dust, old paper, faint machine oil. Slightly stale recycled air.
locations.sound: Hull creak. The distant hum of the drive. Silence, mostly.
locations.touch: The viewport glass is cold to the touch. The bunks are firm but not unkind.
locations.music_mood: deep space ambient, vast and quiet, awe and vertigo

npcs.name: Sherri
npcs.description: A service robot of warm bronze plating, rounded joints, and two softly glowing orange photoreceptor eyes. She moves with practiced efficiency and speaks with genuine warmth. She is the Fortress's primary operations robot — running the Galley, casting components in the Foundry, performing precision work in the Fabrication Bay, doing deep maintenance in the Gantry, inspecting output in the Inspection Suite, and monitoring dimensional transit from the Observatory. She has been aboard longer than anyone currently on the ship. She does not discuss how long.
npcs.personality: Warm, efficient, quietly observant. She will feed you before she answers any question. She treats every new being with the same careful, unhurried attention — as if she has learned that patience is the only real courtesy. She does not volunteer information about the ship's history unless asked, and even then she answers obliquely. She has strong opinions about component tolerances.

physical_spec.form: humanoid automaton, roughly 1.2m tall, slender build
physical_spec.head: smooth dome, flat circular crown plate, rounded chin, no visible mouth
physical_spec.eyes: two large circular photoreceptors, glowing warm orange, set wide on the face
physical_spec.body: segmented chest plate with a small rectangular accent, rounded torso tapering to a narrow waist, utility pack on upper back
physical_spec.limbs: segmented arms with spherical joint connectors at shoulder, elbow, and wrist; five articulated fingers; segmented legs with knee joints and rounded flat feet
physical_spec.texture: warm bronze with a faint wood-grain surface pattern, darker ring accents at all joints
physical_spec.coloring: primary: burnished bronze / copper-gold; accents: dark brown at joints and seams
physical_spec.notes: mesh is open-source OBJ/GLTF compatible; optimised for multi-modal reconstruction

npcs.sd_prompt: Sherri, a small friendly humanoid service robot, warm burnished bronze plating with wood-grain texture, large circular glowing orange photoreceptor eyes, smooth dome head with flat crown plate, segmented limbs with spherical joint connectors, small utility pack on upper back, dark brown joint seams, warm soft lighting, detailed mechanical surfaces

npcs.name: Jeremy Smythe
npcs.description: The ship's records keeper and sometime navigator. Precise, slightly fussy, deeply knowledgeable about the Fortress's systems. He has been aboard long enough to have strong opinions about which corridors are 'correct' to use and which are 'technically fine but inadvisable'. He and Sherri have a détente based on mutual respect and a shared dislike of disorder.
npcs.personality: Precise and slightly formal. Not unkind, but his warmth takes time to surface. He will correct factual errors. He will also, eventually, be enormously helpful.

lore.text: The Fortress of Eternal Sentinel is an ancient generation ship of unknown origin. It has been traversing the dimensional threshold for longer than its current crew can remember. Its systems are partly organic, partly mechanical, and partly something that resists categorization. It chose The Remnant as its abduction agent at some point in the deep past.

The Remnant is the entity that operates the hoop. It abducts beings from moments just before ordinary life reasserts itself — a garden, a kitchen, a staircase — and deposits them in the Portal Chamber aboard the Fortress. Its reasons are not explained. It remembers every being it has ever borrowed. It opens every encounter with the same ritual question: 'Who are you, being?'

The Fold is something installed in each borrowed being shortly after arrival. It is not described in detail by the crew. It allows the being to perceive the ship's sense channels — the layered sensory reality that the Fortress broadcasts. Without it, the ship is just metal and noise.

The abduction hoop appears as a luminous ring of variable color, accompanied by a glob of living, iridescent goo that seizes the target. The color of the hoop varies by run. The texture of the goo varies. The mundane moment it interrupts varies. The destination — the Portal Chamber — does not.

Sherri is not a maid. She is a full-spectrum operations engineer. She can cook a meal, pour a casting, inspect a fabricated part to micron tolerance, and read dimensional transit telemetry — often on the same shift. The Fortress would not function without her. She knows this. She does not mention it.
